disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Pain began around the umbilicus as a dull ache about 8 hours ago, then migrated and localised to the right iliac fossa as sharp intermittent cramps
trigger_question_examples: Where is the pain and where did it start?
How has the pain changed since it began?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It started as a dull ache near my belly button about 8 hours ago, then it moved down and settled in my lower right side as sharp cramps that come and go.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Pain is intermittent and crampy — sharp catches with easing in between, relieved by lying still curled up
trigger_question_examples: Is the pain there all the time or does it come and go?
Does anything make it better or worse?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It comes in sharp waves rather than being constant, and it eases off if I lie curled up on my side.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Two episodes of loose, unformed bowel motions this evening; no blood observed
trigger_question_examples: Any diarrhoea or blood in the stool?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I've been to the toilet twice tonight with loose, unformed motions. I haven't seen any blood.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Background of a long-standing chronic relapsing bowel inflammation with recurrent similar right-sided twinges and intermittent loose motions
trigger_question_examples: Do you have any ongoing bowel or gut conditions?
Have you had pain like this in the same spot before?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yes — I've had a grumbling gut for years. The doctors said it's a long-standing inflammation in my bowel that flares up. I get these same twinges and loose motions on and off, though tonight it's sharper.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No loss of appetite, no persistent vomiting, no rigors or shaking chills
trigger_question_examples: Have you lost your appetite or been vomiting?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: My appetite's okay actually, I haven't been vomiting, and I haven't had any shakes or chills.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Self-measured temperature 37.2 degrees on a home thermometer; patient reports feeling neither hot nor shivery
trigger_question_examples: Have you been able to check your temperature?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I checked with my home thermometer and it said 37.2. I don't feel hot or shivery.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Not currently on any regular immune-modifying or bowel medication; only occasional over-the-counter paracetamol
trigger_question_examples: Are you taking any regular medications for your gut?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No regular medicines for it at the moment — just the odd paracetamol when the twinges come.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Worried the pain represents a bursting appendix
trigger_question_examples: What are you most worried this might be?
Is there something specific you're afraid this could be?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I'm honestly terrified it's my appendix about to burst.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Pain does NOT currently worsen sharply on movement or coughing, and the abdomen does not feel board-hard to the patient
trigger_question_examples: Does it hurt much more if you cough or move suddenly?
Does your tummy feel hard and rigid?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Coughing gives it a small tug but not a huge stab, and my tummy feels sore but not rock-hard.
